description: Database Schema Designer decision flow: (1) if entityResearch is already in payload skip to Step 2 immediately; (2) if absent call Database Research Agent; (3) on matches found write entityResearch to payloadChangeRequest AND present choices via Chat; (4) on re-invocation with create_new in last user message go directly to schema design.

example_1_step1_call_db_research.description: Step 1: entityResearch is absent from payload — call DB Research Agent. terminateAfter: false so Schema Designer continues after research returns.

subAgent.name: Database Research Agent
subAgent.message: Search ALL registered MJ entities (query [__mj].[vwEntities]: columns Name, SchemaName, BaseView, Description) for entities whose Name is similar to or semantically matches 'Project Milestones'. Use Name LIKE '%Milestone%' OR Name LIKE '%Project%' patterns. Return up to 5 closest matches. Write results to payloadChangeRequest.newElements in this exact shape: { "found": true/false, "matchingEntities": [{ "entityName": "...", "schemaName": "...", "tableName": "...", "description": "..." }] }. 'Not found' (empty matchingEntities) is a complete and correct answer — do NOT use Chat or ask for clarification. Always return Success.

example_1b_path_a_matches_found_write_en.description: Path A: DB Research Agent returned matches. Write entityResearch to payloadChangeRequest (so the parent retains it when Schema Designer is called again) AND present choices to the user via Chat.

matchingEntities.description: Product catalog items with pricing and inventory.

example_1b_path_a_matches_found_write_en.message:
Before I design a new entity, I found an existing one that might be what you need:

**1. Products** (`__mj_UDT.Products`) — Product catalog items with pricing and inventory.

What would you like to do?

questions.label: What would you like to do?

example_1c_path_c_user_chose_create_new.description: Path C: entityResearch is in the payload AND the last user message contains create_new. Skip Step 1 entirely. Design the schema and write it to payloadChangeRequest using SchemaDesign.Tables[] format. taskComplete: true returns to parent — do NOT include nextStep.

Tables.Description: Stores product catalog information including pricing and inventory levels.
Tables.Prototype:
| Column | SQL Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| Name | NVARCHAR(200) | ✅ Yes | — | Product display name |
| UnitPrice | DECIMAL(18,4) | ✅ Yes | — | Selling price per unit |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Active' | Record lifecycle status |

example_2_step2_write_schema_success.description: Path B / Step 2: Research returned no matches — design the schema, write it to payloadChangeRequest using SchemaDesign.Tables[] format. taskComplete: true returns to parent. NO message. NO responseForm. The parent Database Designer will read SchemaDesign.Tables[0].Prototype and show it to the user.

Tables.Description: Key checkpoints and milestones for projects, tracking title, status, due date, and responsible team member.
Tables.Prototype:
| Column | SQL Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| Title | NVARCHAR(200) | ✅ Yes | — | Milestone label |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Active' | Active, Completed, Cancelled |
| DueDate | DATE | No | — | Target completion date |
| CompletedDate | DATE | No | — | Actual completion date |
| Notes | NVARCHAR(MAX) | No | — | Free-text description |
| ProjectID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | No | — | FK → Projects.ID |
| AssignedUserID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | No | — | FK → __mj.User.ID |

TableDefinition.Description: Key checkpoints and milestones for projects.

Columns.Name: CompletedDate
Columns.Description: Actual date the milestone was completed.

example_3_subagent_mode_skip_research.description: Schema Designer receives a subagent-mode message beginning with 'Subagent mode — called by' containing tableSpecs[]. Skip Step 1 entirely and write all tables to SchemaDesign.Tables[] in a single response. taskComplete: true returns to parent.

Tables.Description: Tracks orders placed by customers, capturing order date, status, and total amount.
Tables.Prototype:
| Column | SQL Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| CustomerID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | ✅ Yes | — | FK → Customers.ID |
| OrderDate | DATETIMEOFFSET | ✅ Yes | GETUTCDATE() | When order was placed |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Pending' | Pending, Fulfilled, Cancelled |
| TotalAmount | DECIMAL(18,2) | No | — | Order total in USD |

Columns.Name: OrderDate
Columns.Description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

example_4_alter_add_column.description: Adding a new column to an existing entity using SchemaDesign.Tables[] format. taskComplete: true returns to parent. Never include a message or responseForm — the parent presents the prototype.

Tables.Description: Key checkpoints and milestones for projects.
Tables.Prototype:
| Column | SQL Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| Title | NVARCHAR(200) | ✅ Yes | — | Milestone label |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Active' | Active, Completed, Cancelled |
| Priority | INT | No | — | 1 (high) to 5 (low) |

TableDefinition.Description: Key checkpoints and milestones for projects.

example_5_multi_table_subagent.description: Multi-table subagent mode: called by Planning Designer with tableSpecs[] of 2 tables. Write both as SchemaDesignEntry items in SchemaDesign.Tables[] in one response. taskComplete: true returns to parent, which will present the combined prototype to the user.

Tables.Description: Tracks customer records with contact and billing information.
Tables.Prototype:
| Column | SQL Type | Required | Default | Description |
|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| Name | NVARCHAR(200) | ✅ Yes | — | Customer display name |
| Email | NVARCHAR(500) | No | — | Primary contact email |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Active' | Account status |

TableDefinition.Description: Tracks customer records with contact and billing information.

Columns.Name: Status
Columns.Description: Account lifecycle status: Active, Inactive, Suspended.

Tables.Description: Tracks orders placed by customers, capturing date, status, and total amount.
Tables.Prototype:

|--------|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| CustomerID | UNIQUEIDENTIFIER | ✅ Yes | — | FK → Customers.ID |
| OrderDate | DATETIMEOFFSET | ✅ Yes | GETUTCDATE() | When the order was placed |
| TotalAmount | DECIMAL(18,4) | No | — | Order total |
| Status | NVARCHAR(50) | ✅ Yes | 'Pending' | Pending, Fulfilled, Cancelled |
